SQL script to initialize a database with `user` and `post` tables, dropping existing data first.

# Purpose
This SQL script initializes a database by first removing any existing `user` and `post` tables and then creating new, empty tables with the same names. The `user` table includes columns for `id`, `username`, and `password`, with `id` set as the primary key and `username` required to be unique. The `post` table includes columns for `id`, `author_id`, `created`, `title`, and `body`, with `id` as the primary key and `author_id` as a foreign key referencing the `id` in the `user` table. The `created` column in the `post` table is automatically set to the current timestamp by default. This script is intended for setting up the initial structure of a database for a system that manages users and their posts.
